title: Acknowledgements, Delivery Tags, Redelivery, and Effect Ownership · Inference Brief: Delivery Tag Channel Scope
summary: A handler acknowledges on a different channel, auto-acks before validation, nacks every error with requeue, treats redelivered false as first-ever processing, and double-acks after a timeout race. Planned artifact: Add and verify the delivery tag channel scope increment in delivery ownership and acknowledgement decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Acknowledge, nack, or reject a delivery exactly once on its owning channel and prevent stale or cross-channel tag use.
Choose manual or automatic acknowledgement from loss tolerance, consumer memory, work duration, and effect ownership.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Delivery Tag Channel Scope · Theory Delivery Tag Channel Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5798 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce same-generation, exactly-one terminal acknowledgement after durable effect; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Delivery Tag Channel Scope contract for the supplied data.
